key_issues: All 7 BLOCKING fixes from iteration-001 applied and correct: (1) two differently-built stub providers proving the swap seam through an identical validate/result surface; (2) zero-error result enforced via struct #:guard with a falsifiable check-exn test; (3) value matrix (json-null/hasheq/list/string/number) round-tripping equal?; (4) handle reuse compile-once/validate-many; (5) two independent handles from the same provider; (6) banned-set AC now includes racket/port matching the shipped item-008 helper; (7) closed-set negatives (validation-result? 42 -> #f, bare error element is not a result).

All 6 SUGGESTED fixes applied: compile-on-garbage fail-fast policy pinned; >=2-error order test; mixed string/integer path '("items" 0 "name"); negative-accessor raises + non-jsexpr policy; cross-provider handle totality noted as vacuous under recommended encoding; portability walk entry-point=provider.rkt + (module+ test ...) scope-limit restated.

New construct correctly handled: the validation-errors #:guard does not conflict with handle opacity (it lives on a transparent result struct, not the opaque handle), and the struct ordering dependency (validation-error must be defined before validation-errors) is flagged in three places (inline comment, Implementation Step 3, Decisions (b)).

No internal inconsistency: the iter-001-class banned-set mismatch is resolved; Description/AC/Testing Strategy/Manual Checklist/Validation Results all agree; every acceptance criterion maps to a concrete named test.

Cross-refs verified live: progress.md line 71 deliverable text matches the Completion Reminder cite; queue-002 item 010 sanctions the two-op split; item-008 helper (mcp/core/test/main-test.rkt) confirms the banned set and the module+test scope limit.
Residual items are non-blocking nitpicks only (always-hold value-matrix stub choice, encoding-specific REPL one-liner already caveated, json-null parameterization awareness).
